title: Crispy Argentinian Cauliflower Stirfry
description: A crunchy, lowcarb Argentinian-inspired cauliflower dish seasoned with rice vinegar and chimichurristyle herbs.

ingredients.name: Rice vinegar
ingredients.notes: seasoned with a pinch of salt and pepper

instructions: Preheat the air fryer to 400°F (200°C). Lightly coat cauliflower florets with olive oil and season with salt, pepper, ground cumin, and smoked paprika.
Air‑fry the cauliflower for 15 minutes, shaking the basket halfway, until golden and crisp. Remove and set aside.
Meanwhile, heat a splash of olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Sauté diced onion until translucent, then add minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds.
Add sliced red bell pepper to the skillet and cook for 3–4 minutes until slightly softened.
Stir in tamari soy sauce, lime juice, and rice vinegar, cooking for another minute to meld flavors.
Return the air‑fried cauliflower to the skillet, tossing gently to coat with the sauce. Cook for 2 minutes, letting the cauliflower absorb the flavors.
Remove the skillet from heat and stir in chopped fresh parsley and cilantro.
If desired, sprinkle sesame seeds on top for added crunch.
Plate the dish immediately, garnishing with extra herbs if desired.
Serve hot as a satisfying low‑carb lunch or dinner.
